texto: There, Clover found the "gardens and great trees and old cottages...so beautiful" that seeing them exhausted her. It was as if, she joked with her husband, "this English world is a huge stage-play got up only to amuse Americans. It is obviously unreal, eccentric, and taken out of novels.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Natalie Dykstra: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “There, Clover found the "gardens and great trees and old cottages...so beautiful" that seeing the…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amor, england, travel pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e travel.

frase_original: There, Clover found the "gardens and great trees and old cottages...so beautiful" that seeing them exhausted her. It was as if, she joked with her husband, "this English world is a huge stage-play got up only to amuse Americans. It is obviously unreal, eccentric, and taken out of novels.
